label: ai
topic: How to start unit testing JavaScript code
text:
If you have been writing JavaScript without tests, you are not alone. Testing is one of those practices most developers acknowledge as important but many avoid because the learning curve feels steep. You have working code — why spend time writing more code to check it?

The answer becomes clear the first time you refactor a function and break something three files away. Tests are not about proving your code works right now. They are about making sure it still works after you change it.

Types of Testing

Unit tests verify that individual functions or modules behave correctly in isolation. If you have a function that calculates sales tax, a unit test confirms it returns the right value for a given input. They are small, fast, and focused on a single piece of logic.

Integration tests verify that multiple units work together. If your tax function feeds into an order total function, an integration test checks the complete pipeline. These are broader and may involve databases or API calls.

Functional tests (sometimes called end-to-end tests) verify the application from the user's perspective, simulating real actions like clicking buttons and filling forms. These are the slowest and most complex to maintain.

For developers starting out, unit tests offer the best ratio of effort to value.

Why Test?

Tests serve as documentation. A well-written test describes what a function is supposed to do with concrete examples. When another developer needs to understand expected behaviour, the test suite is often more informative than comments.

Tests enable confident refactoring. Codebases without tests accumulate technical debt because developers are afraid to change existing code. With a test suite, you can restructure knowing that breakage will be caught immediately.

Tests encourage better design. Code that is difficult to test is usually difficult to maintain. Writing tests reveals problems — functions doing too many things, tangled logic, excessive dependencies — and pushes you toward smaller, more focused functions with clear inputs and outputs.

Getting Started with Jest

Jest is a popular JavaScript testing framework maintained by Meta. It requires minimal configuration, includes a built-in assertion library and mocking utilities, and works with both front-end and back-end code.

Install it with npm install --save-dev jest, then add a test script to your package.json: "test": "jest". Run your tests with npm test.

Jest looks for files ending in .test.js, files ending in .spec.js, or files inside a __tests__ directory. Consistency within a project matters more than which convention you pick.

A basic test file imports the function you want to test, then uses describe to group related tests and it (or test) to define individual cases. Inside each test, you call the function with specific inputs and use expect with a matcher to verify the output.

For example, if you have an add function, your test calls add(2, 3) and asserts the result equals 5 using toBe. For objects and arrays, use toEqual, which performs a deep comparison.

Jest provides a rich set of matchers: toBeTruthy and toBeFalsy for boolean checks, toContain for arrays and strings, toThrow for expected errors, and toBeGreaterThan for numeric comparisons. They read almost like English, which is intentional.

Practical Tips

Start with pure functions — those that take inputs and return outputs without side effects. These are the easiest to test. As you grow comfortable, introduce mocking for more complex interactions.

Write tests before you fix bugs. When you find a bug, write a test that reproduces it, then fix it and watch the test pass. This guarantees the bug will not silently return.

Keep tests independent. Each test should set up its own data and not depend on execution order. Shared state between tests is a common source of flaky suites.

Testing is a skill that improves with practice. Your first tests will feel clumsy. The investment pays off the first time your suite catches a regression that would have reached production.
